channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mad Cool Festival 2026 Concludes with Stellar Performances by Pulp, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, and David Byrne
item.description: The 10th-anniversary edition of the Mad Cool Festival in Madrid concluded with a spectacular final day of performances on July 11, 2026. The day featured a diverse lineup including rising stars and es...

item.title: Diesel and kerosene prices rise due to Middle East tensions
item.description: The Philippine government announced a significant increase in diesel and kerosene prices starting July 14, 2026, driven by escalating tensions in the Middle East. Energy Secretary Lance Spencer Yu rev...

item.title: Art programs empower First Nations women in Australian prisons through creativity and resilience
item.description: The article explores how art initiatives in Australian prisons are fostering resilience and empowerment among First Nations women. Programs like the Y Write Prison Education Project in the Northern Te...

item.title: DFAT raises travel warnings for UAE and Qatar amid heightened regional conflict
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT) has upgraded its travel advisories for the United Arab Emirates and Qatar, warning Australians to 'reconsider their need to travel' due to escalating...

item.title: FIFA President Proposes Expanding World Cup to 64 Teams for 2030 Tournament
item.description: Gianni Infantino, President of FIFA, has indicated that the organisation may consider expanding the World Cup to 64 teams ahead of the 2030 tournament, which will be hosted across Spain, Portugal, and...

item.title: US Senator Mitch McConnell delays Senate return due to health issues
item.description: US Senator Mitch McConnell, a prominent Republican from Kentucky, has announced that he will not be able to return to the Senate floor for voting procedures soon due to ongoing recovery from a fall an...

item.title: Alberta Referendum: Carney Urges Albertans to Support Provincial Leadership Over Exit
item.description: The article highlights Alberta Premier Danielle Smith’s (Carney) call for Albertans to prioritize provincial leadership over potential separation during a visit to the Calgary Stampede. Smith emphasiz...

item.title: Raila Odinga Junior Signals Possible 2027 Political Run, Calls for Luo Unity
item.description: Raila Odinga Junior has hinted at entering elective politics ahead of the 2027 General Election, seeking support from Kenyans during a church fundraiser in Homa Bay. Speaking alongside his mother, Mam...

item.title: Togo strengthens border defenses and forms alliances against jihadist threats
item.description: Togo has been secretly fortifying its border with Burkina Faso to combat jihadist groups from the Sahel. The country has constructed a long trench and established military bases to monitor and block j...

item.title: Mayon Volcano shows signs of increased activity due to high sulfur dioxide levels
item.description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) has issued warnings about potential volcanic activity at Mayon Volcano following a significant increase in sulfur dioxide emissions. T...

item.title: Exploring Innovations in Robotics, Nature, and Design: July 5–12, 2026
item.description: The article highlights a variety of topics spanning technology, nature, and design. It begins with a discussion about Weave Robotics' Isaac, an $8,000 laundry folding robot, raising questions about tr...

item.title: Tropical Depression Haishen Develops East of Philippines, Still Outside PAR
item.description: A low-pressure area tracked by PAGASA strengthened into a tropical depression named Haishen on July 13, 2026. As of 8 a.m., Haishen was located 1,275 kilometers east of Eastern Visayas with maximum su...

item.title: Linda Mwananchi's Movement Reshapes Kenyan Politics
item.description: The article explores how Linda Mwananchi has emerged as a transformative political force in Kenya, uniting generations through shared ideals rather than personal loyalty. It challenges traditional pow...

item.title: Sudan court sentences RSF leader Hemeti to death for war crimes
item.description: A Sudanese court has sentenced Mohamed Hamdan 'Hemeti' Dagalo, the leader of the Rapid Support Forces (RSF), and 15 others to death in absentia for their roles in killing regional governor Khamis Abba...

item.title: FG closes illegal mining site in Osun after arresting suspects
item.description: The Federal Government (FG) has taken action against illegal mining activities in Osun State by arresting two suspects and shutting down the affected mining site. The operation, carried out by federal...

item.title: Regina Roughriders dominate Ti-Cats with 38-7 victory
item.description: The Regina Roughriders delivered a commanding performance in their matchup against the Ti-Cats, overcoming a sluggish offensive start to secure a decisive 38-7 win. The game, played on July 12, 2026, ...

item.title: Central US Experiences Record-Breaking Heatwave
item.description: A severe heatwave has broken temperature records across the central United States, prompting widespread concerns about public health and infrastructure resilience. The unprecedented weather event, cha...

item.title: Nigerian universities expand captive power generation as unreliable national grid pushes institutions towards self-generation
item.description: Nigerian universities are increasingly investing in their own electricity generation as the country's persistent power supply challenges continue to affect teaching, research and daily campus operatio...

item.title: Prime Minister Carney Promotes Pipeline Development Amid Alberta Energy Discussions
item.description: Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's chief of staff, David Carney, addressed energy policy and pipeline development during his appearance at the Calgary Stampede, emphasizing collaboration across provinces...

item.title: Heatwaves in the UK May Have Led to Thousands of Deaths
item.description: Experts estimate that over 2,700 people may have died from heat-related causes during the UK's exceptionally hot weather in May and June. The figures, from Imperial College London, the Met Office, and...
